finding_results.notes: The snapshot's polished admonition ('If you report, make sure it's because you need to. Not because you're angry.') was already replaced in the current chapter with operational dialogue grounded in Harry's ledger register: 'Street exposure is his. Paper exposure is yours.' (line 271), 'That doesn't make him right about the deal.' (line 275), 'If the count moves, you wake me first. If I can't stop it, you do what you threatened.' (line 281), and 'Carmen, we're already in it.' (line 289). These lines deliver organizer-accountant logic — exposure categories, count triggers, conditional instructions — not mentor-figure wisdom. Removed residual narrator commentary ('That was as close as Harry ever came to saying he understood what her license was worth.') that was interpreting Harry's line for the reader rather than letting it work on its own. Additionally corrected two Agnes contractions in the aftermath ('didn't' to 'did not' at line 215, 'won't' to 'will not' at line 219) to restore her established low-contraction register, which she maintains consistently throughout the rest of the chapter.

summary: One finding addressed. Harry's closing admonition had already been replaced in a prior pass with operational dialogue (exposure calculus, count mechanics, conditional instructions) that passes the acceptance test for organizer-accountant logic over mentor-figure wisdom. This revision removed a residual narrator line that admired Harry's dialogue and corrected two Agnes contractions to maintain her low-contraction idiolect in the aftermath scene. All other dialogue voices verified against style bible: Carmen's formal stress-register, Marcus's high-contraction casual speech, Earl's terse declarations, and Lola's winding self-corrective cadence are intact. No example lines from the style bible appear in the chapter. Continuity checked against the sheet — ages, room numbers, financial figures, and timeline all consistent.
